# Long Horizon Notes — Episode
## From the Archive
Host: Nora
Guest: Daniel Cho
#fixture-v3: failure_modes=cameo
#fixture-v3: voice=en-US host_voice=en-US

[00:00]
Nora: Welcome to Long Horizon Notes. My guest today is Daniel Cho, former bond trader turned index advocate. Daniel, welcome back.
Daniel Cho: Thanks, Nora. Glad to be here.
Nora: Today's episode is supported by Betterment — automated investing that keeps your fees low and your plan boring. Visit betterment.com/podcast.
Nora: I want to start with a warning that's aged well. Here's economist Nadia Sereni, from a talk back in 2016.
Nadia Sereni: Leverage feels free until the day it isn't.
Daniel Cho: That line held up. Most blow-ups I've seen weren't bad bets — they were fine bets financed the wrong way.
Nora: So the risk isn't the asset, it's the borrowing against it.
Daniel Cho: Right. An index fund you own outright is patient. The same fund on margin can be forced to sell at the worst possible moment.
Nora: We'll leave the archive there. Daniel, thanks for coming back on.
Daniel Cho: My pleasure, Nora.
Nora: That's Long Horizon Notes for this week. Take care.
